title: Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake and Ann Michael Maye attended a Bruins game at TD Garden, witnessing a 6-3 win over the Dallas Stars.
Drake Maye saluted a fan wearing an 'I [Heart] Drake Maye' t-shirt, making a heart sign on the Jumbotron.
Earlier, Drake Maye participated in the 'Saving by Shaving' event, raising over $4 million for Boston Children's Hospital.

title: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Doc Rivers, former Celtics coach, inducted into 2026 Basketball Hall of Fame, joining Amar'e Stoudemire, Candace Parker, Elena Delle Donne.
Rivers led Celtics to 2008 NBA Championship and 2010 NBA Finals, coaching Boston from 2004 to 2013.
Over 27 seasons, Rivers achieved a 1,191-861 record, one of only 11 coaches with 1,000+ career wins.

title: Drake Maye shaves his head as part of charity event benefiting Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye shaved his head for a charity event benefiting Boston Children's Hospital, raising $4.15 million.
Granite Telecommunications donated $2,500 per shaved head or hair donation, with CEO Rob Hale matching the funds.
The Mayes launched a foundation to support children and families in New England and North Carolina.

title: Boston police officer criminally charged after allegedly leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Police Officer Sean Cullen charged after leaving loaded department-issued gun unsecured in his vehicle.
Cullen faces felony and misdemeanor charges following a domestic dispute that led to the discovery of unsecured firearms.
Cullen's police certification suspended as protocol for officers charged with a felony; he earned $195,000 last year.

title: N.H. governor’s security aid rescues Mass. driver from fiery toll booth crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Gov. Kelly Ayotte and a security trooper helped rescue a driver from a burning car at Bedford Toll Plaza in N.H.
The crash occurred on the Everett Turnpike, causing serious injuries to the sole occupant who was hospitalized.
The tolls were temporarily closed for investigation, and the accident remains under investigation by state police.

title: Mike Vrabel talks A.J. Brown, Patriots draft, and other takeaways from NFL owners’ meetings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel discusses potential acquisition of Eagles receiver A.J. Brown but notes trade unlikely before June 1.
Vrabel emphasizes continued growth for quarterback Drake Maye and outlines high goals for coming season, including winning championships.
New England Patriots aim to strengthen roster in 2026 NFL Draft with focus on best player available, not specific position needs.

title: Mass. man charged with murdering wife, allegedly told 911 operator ‘my gun went off’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mark Opper, 56, charged with murder in the shooting death of his wife Diane Opper at their Belchertown home.
Opper allegedly told a 911 operator his gun went off; police found multiple gunshot wounds on Diane's torso.
Opper held without bail; investigation found evidence supporting intentional shooting; court hearing set for April 27.

title: Brockton man facing 14 charges after allegedly engaging in shootout with police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jailson Andrade, 44, of Brockton faces 14 charges, including assault to murder, after a shootout with police.
The incident occurred after a police chase that ended at the intersection of Spring and Moraine Streets.
Andrade was held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing set for May 1 in Brockton District Court.

title: 1 killed, 1 injured in Charlton crash, driver reportedly refused to stop for police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fatal two-vehicle crash occurred on Route 20 in Charlton, Massachusetts early Tuesday morning.
One driver was killed and another injured after a pick-up truck reportedly refused a police stop.
Charlton and Massachusetts State Police are investigating the incident; the crash closed the highway temporarily.

title: Victim identified in fatal Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Stewart, 47, from Worcester was identified as the victim in a Roxbury hit-and-run on March 20.
The hit-and-run occurred near Southampton Street and Bradston Street, and the driver fled the scene.
No arrests have been made regarding the incident, which remains under investigation by Boston police.
